unit.name: Conditional diffusion, CFG, and noise schedules
unit.description: Explains conditional rectified flow, classifier-free guidance, and why non-uniform noise schedules are used.
unit.summary: Conditional rectified flow extends the dataset from x to pairs (x,y) and gives y as an auxiliary model input, so sampling can be controlled by a user-provided condition such as text or an image [ts=2730s], [ts=2759s]. Because naïve conditional models may not obey the conditioning strongly enough, classifier-free guidance (CFG) modifies training by randomly dropping the condition, often about 50% of the time [ts=2806s], [ts=2823s]. This forces a single model to learn both unconditional velocity vectors v_empty toward p(x) and conditional velocity vectors v_y toward p(x|y) [ts=2864s], [ts=2881s]. During sampling, CFG forms v_cfg=(1+w)v_y - w v_empty so larger w overemphasizes the conditioning signal [ts=2904s], [ts=2937s]. The cost is that each sampling step usually evaluates the model twice, doubling inference cost [ts=2950s]. The lecture then discusses noise schedules: uniform sampling over t gives equal emphasis to easy endpoints and hard middle-noise cases [ts=3008s], [ts=3053s]. Practical schedules such as logit-normal sampling put more weight in the middle, and shifted schedules help high-resolution data where pixel correlations change how much noise is needed [ts=3090s], [ts=3113s].

key_points.text: Conditional rectified flow trains on pairs (x,y) and feeds y as an auxiliary input.
Classifier-free guidance randomly drops the conditioning signal during training.
Dropping the condition forces the model to learn an unconditional velocity vector toward p(x).
Keeping the condition gives a conditional velocity vector toward p(x|y).
CFG uses v_cfg=(1+w)v_y - w v_empty to emphasize conditioning.
CFG typically doubles sampling cost because the model is evaluated twice per step.
Uniform t sampling gives equal weight to easy endpoints and hard middle-noise cases.
Logit-normal and shifted noise schedules put more training emphasis where denoising is harder or resolution-dependent.

kp_catalog.name: Conditional rectified flow
kp_catalog.description: Conditioning adds y as an input so generation can follow labels, text, images, or other user controls.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because conditional rectified flow clarifies conditioning adds y as an input so generation can follow labels, text, images, or other user controls, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Classifier-free guidance
kp_catalog.description: CFG combines unconditional and conditional predictions to strengthen adherence to conditioning at sampling time.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because classifier-free guidance clarifies cFG combines unconditional and conditional predictions to strengthen adherence to conditioning at sampling time, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Noise schedules
kp_catalog.description: Non-uniform t sampling such as logit-normal or shifted schedules emphasizes difficult noise levels and high-resolution needs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because noise schedules clarifies non-uniform t sampling such as logit-normal or shifted schedules emphasizes difficult noise levels and high-resolution needs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
